name: Miño de Medinaceli — circular desde Medinaceli
region_intro: Touring cycling around Medinaceli offers a diverse landscape, characterized by its hilltop position in the Sierra Ministra, providing expansive views of the Arbujuelo and Jalón valleys. The region features impressive rocky walls of the Jalón River Gorges, contrasting with vast cereal fields and open plateaus. Routes often include climbs, rewarding cyclists with panoramic vistas across the historic Spanish landscape. Best touring cycling routes around Medinaceli The most popular touring cycling route is Miño de Medinaceli — circular desde Medinaceli, a 17.8 miles (28.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This circular route connects the historic town of Medinaceli with the charming village of Miño de Medinaceli. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Tierras de Frontera Loop, a difficult 30.6 miles (49.2 km) path. This route traverses historic Spanish landscapes with notable elevation changes, offering a challenging ride through varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Anguita Village and Church – Medinaceli loop from Estación de Torralba, a 70.0 miles (112.6 km) trail leading through historic villages and cultural landmarks, often completed in about 8 hours 59 minutes. Touring cycling around Medinaceli is defined by panoramic valley views, dramatic river gorges, and expansive cereal fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Medinaceli are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 50 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Medinaceli's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Medinaceli?
A: There are over a dozen touring cycling routes around Medinaceli, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has rated these routes highly, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in Medinaceli?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in our current selection, many of the 'moderate' routes can be suitable for families or those seeking a less strenuous ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041736284/mino-de-medinaceli-circular-desde-medinaceli" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miño de Medinaceli — circular desde Medinaceli&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 17.8-mile (28.7 km) circular path that connects historic towns and offers a pleasant experience.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Medinaceli?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041736284/mino-de-medinaceli-circular-desde-medinaceli" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miño de Medinaceli — circular desde Medinaceli&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 17.8-mile (28.7 km) loop. Another is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041738665/tierras-de-frontera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierras de Frontera Loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 30.6 miles (49.2 km) with significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of scenic views can I expect on touring cycling routes around Medinaceli?
A: Touring cycling around Medinaceli offers diverse and panoramic views. You'll encounter expansive vistas of the Arbujuelo and Jalón valleys from the hilltop town, dramatic rocky walls of the Jalón River Gorges, and vast cereal fields contrasting with open plateaus. Routes often involve climbs that reward cyclists with spectacular scenery.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Medinaceli offers several difficult routes for experienced cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041738665/tierras-de-frontera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierras de Frontera Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 30.6-mile (49.2 km) path with notable elevation changes. For an even longer and more demanding ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguita Village and Church – Medinaceli loop from Estación de Torralba&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 70.0 miles (112.6 km) and includes significant elevation gains.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for touring cycling routes in Medinaceli?
A: Routes vary significantly in length and duration. You can find shorter rides like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041736284/mino-de-medinaceli-circular-desde-medinaceli" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miño de Medinaceli — circular desde Medinaceli&lt;/a&gt; at 17.8 miles (28.7 km), taking under 2 hours. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguita Village and Church – Medinaceli loop from Estación de Torralba&lt;/a&gt;, can be up to 70.0 miles (112.6 km) and take around 9 hours to complete.

Q: What historical attractions or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Medinaceli is rich in history. You can incorporate visits to sites like the Roman Triumphal Arch, the Arab Gate, and the Palacio Ducal de Medinaceli. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguita Village and Church – Medinaceli loop from Estación de Torralba&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through historic villages and cultural landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5572415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid near Arcos de Jalón&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant historical trail in the region.

Q: Are there any natural attractions like gorges or waterfalls near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is known for its natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5041533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jalón River Gorges&lt;/a&gt;, stretching from Somaén to Jubera and Lodares, feature impressive rocky walls and are home to birds of prey. You can also find waterfalls such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6020877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Cuerda&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6020795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chorronera del Río Blanco&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6886202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arbujuelo Springs and Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the touring cycling routes?
A: Medinaceli itself offers the historic Plaza Mayor, known as one of the most beautiful squares in Old Castile, which is an ideal spot for a break with its characteristic arcades, implying cafes and restaurants. Many routes also pass through charming villages where you can find local establishments.

Q: Can I expect to see any wildlife while cycling in Medinaceli?
A: The natural landscapes around Medinaceli, particularly the Jalón River Gorges, are known for hosting birds of prey, offering opportunities for wildlife spotting during your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Medinaceli?
A: While specific recommendations aren't provided, the region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, and winters might be chilly, but cycling is possible year-round depending on personal preference and weather conditions.

Q: Is Medinaceli accessible by public transport for cyclists?
A: Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguita Village and Church – Medinaceli loop from Estación de Torralba&lt;/a&gt;, indicate starting points accessible by train (Estación de Torralba). This suggests that public transport options, particularly train services, might be available to reach the general area, allowing cyclists to start their tours from various points.
tips: Medinaceli is a place that you should not miss on any bike tour in this region, because even if the climb up the city hill is a bit tedious - it's worth it! You will be greeted by an ancient Roman triumphal arch. It probably dates back to the 1st century and is the city's landmark. The Plaza Mayor is also considered one of the most beautiful squares in Old Castile and invites you to have a coffee under the arcades. The famous song about the Spanish national hero El Cid also mentions the place as an important border crossing for the knight on his journeys between Castile and Valencia. And from the castle hill you can enjoy a beautiful sunset over the vast plains of Castile in the evening.

Miño de Medinaceli is a small rural town in the province of Soria. You can park next to the community pools and start your walk. Climb up to see the ruins of a Moorish-occupied castle dating back to the 10th century and sitting on top of a hill above the city.
Imposing monumental square in a town that deserves to stop for a visit
